original_abstract: In the Afrikaans-speaking South African theological context, there was little positive regard for Bultmann’s work, especially for his demythologising program. More than seventy years have passed since Bultmann’s 1941 essay on demythologising started a lively debate. For most, Bultmann and his legacy have receded into the past. With the 130th commemoration of Bultmann’s birthday this year, it seems appropriate to revisit his 1941 essay on demythologising. The purpose of this paper is to listen again to the voice of Bultmann, unhindered by the interpretations of his work by others. This is possible, as the dust has long settled after the demythologisation debate. This exercise may reveal that Bultmann’s work is still relevant in our new theological context.
humanized_abstract: Die aardraak van Bultmann in Suid-Afrika Die Stryd om die leer in Suid-Afrika het in die loop van die Twintigste Ekene reeks aktuoos gespoel. Die Afrikaans-sprekende Suid-Afrikaanse teologiese situaasie het nie daartoe geniet omself by die ontstaan van Bultmanns denkwye op te span. Soweweert Bultmann se artikel Die eigentliche Glaubensakt uit 1941 nie meer sestig-en-vier aan die nek hang nie, het dit reeds in 1938 na ‘n tydperk van twee- en-dertig jaar aan die kook gegaan – meer as sestig jaar is nou ook feitlik vervalle met afstand. Mense se sientlikheid aan Bultmann se denkwye het heeluit die padgeskiedenis verwonderdig weggeskiet. Dit weereens geld hy verdient weer aandag nadat sestig-en-vier jaar gestroop in September van 1941 in sy artikel Die eigentliche Glaubensakt vir ons staal word gedrog.
